editableGoldMemories.label: UNMAPPED
editableGoldMemories.value: Regularly seeks professional rephrasing and grammatical improvements for engineering-related resume bullet points.

editableNewDialogue.text: Please rephrase this in a professional way for a resume: "Coordinated with subcontractors to close snags and support final handover."
Coordinated with subcontractors to close outstanding snags and support the successful final handover of the project.
Make it shorter and more action-oriented.
Drove snag closure with subcontractors to enable timely project handover.
Rewrite this too: "Prepared method statements and risk assessments for mechanical installation works."
Developed method statements and risk assessments (MSRA) for mechanical installation activities.
Also, what does MSRA stand for?
MSRA commonly stands for Method Statement and Risk Assessment.
Give one more alternative version without abbreviations.
Prepared method statements and risk assessments to ensure safe and compliant mechanical installation works.
Support the QA/QC team during MEP final handover and authority submissions—rephrase it professionally for my CV.
Provided support to the QA/QC team during MEP final handover, including the preparation and submission of documentation for client review and relevant authority approvals.
In the last point, please don’t write “Mechanical Site Engineer.” I’m actually a Mechanical Project Engineer, so update the sentence accordingly.

As a Mechanical Project Engineer, I oversaw and supervised all site activities, including managing the labor force, to ensure that the execution work adhered to the project's quality plan. Additionally, I provided assistance to the site mechanical engineer for the assigned project, specifically in documentation and related tasks.
